title: Learning from Home: The New Way to Study
passage:
Learning Online from Home

Today, many students do not go to a traditional school building every day. Instead, they study online from their homes. This is called online learning or distance education. To start learning, students only need a computer, a quiet room, and a good internet connection.

In an online school, students watch video lessons on their computer screens. Teachers write simple lessons and put them on a special website. Students can read these lessons, watch the videos, and do their homework. When they finish their work, they send it to their teachers by email. Some classes also use live video calls. In these calls, students can see their teachers and classmates. They can ask questions and talk about their school subjects easily.

Online learning has many good points. First, students can choose their own study time. They do not have to wake up very early in the morning to catch a school bus. Second, they can study at their own speed. If they do not understand a lesson, they can watch the video again. However, online learning also has some problems. Sometimes, the internet connection is weak, and the video stops. Also, some students feel lonely because they do not see their friends. They miss playing games in the schoolyard during lunch breaks.

Let's look at Mark, a 10-year-old student from Australia. Mark lives on a big farm, and the nearest school is two hours away by car. Every morning, Mark sits at his desk and turns on his computer. He meets his teacher, Miss Smith, and five other classmates on a video call. Mark says, 'I love online school because I can study and still help my parents on the farm in the afternoon.' For Mark, distance education is the perfect way to learn.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, or C.
questions.text: What do students need to start online learning?

options.B: A computer and a good internet connection.
options.C: A big classroom and a uniform.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'To start learning, students only need a computer, a quiet room, and a good internet connection.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, or C.
questions.text: How do online students send their finished homework to teachers?

options.C: They give it to them in person.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'When they finish their work, they send it to their teachers by email.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, or C.
questions.text: Why do some students feel lonely during online learning?

options.B: They do not see their friends.
options.C: They do not like their teachers.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'some students feel lonely because they do not see their friends.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, or C.
questions.text: How far is the nearest school from Mark's home?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Mark lives on a big farm, and the nearest school is two hours away by car.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Teachers write simple lessons and put them on a special website.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Teachers write simple lessons and put them on a special website.' This matches the statement exactly.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Online students must wake up early to catch a school bus.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'They do not have to wake up very early in the morning to catch a school bus.' Therefore, the statement is false.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Mark is a ten-year-old student living in Canada.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Mark, a 10-year-old student from Australia.' He lives in Australia, not Canada.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Miss Smith teaches science to Mark and his classmates.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 mentions that Mark meets his teacher, Miss Smith, but it does not mention what subject she teaches.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: If online students do not understand a lesson, they can watch the ______ again.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'If they do not understand a lesson, they can watch the video again.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: When the ______ connection is weak, the online video stops.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Sometimes, the internet connection is weak, and the video stops.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Mark enjoys online school because he can help his ______ in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Mark says, "I love online school because I can study and still help my parents on the farm in the afternoon."'

vocabulary.meaning: following ideas or methods that have existed for a long time
a system of education where students study at home using the internet
sad because you are not with other people
an open area next to a school where children can play

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt điểm cao (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ này, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ khóa nổi bật như 'email', 'lonely', 'Australia', 'Mark' trong bài để tìm ra đáp án nhanh nhất.

2. Điền từ chính xác: Đối với dạng điền từ (Sentence Completion), hãy chú ý giới hạn từ (không quá 2 từ). Bạn phải copy chính xác từ trong bài đọc, không được thay đổi dạng từ (ví dụ: giữ nguyên số ít/số nhiều).

3. Phân biệt False và Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi ngược lại hoàn toàn với bài đọc, chọn FALSE. Nếu bài đọc không nhắc đến chi tiết đó (ví dụ như môn học của cô giáo Smith), chọn NOT GIVEN.
